semantic.role: Defines extraction rules for code elements and usage patterns for the ArchSpine mirror system.
responsibilities: Describing how to import code entities
Specifying export patterns for functions, structs, traits, implementations, and variables
Specifying usage patterns for function calls and path accesses
outOfScope: Documentation of the overall ArchSpine system architecture
User guide for using the mirror system
Implementation details of the parsing engine

English.purpose: This document serves as a configuration schema for defining how code symbols are extracted from source code within the ArchSpine mirror system. It specifies pattern-based rules for identifying imports, exports (such as functions, structs, traits, implementations, and variables), and usages (like function calls and path accesses). The patterns are based on tree-sitter grammar constructs.
English.context_and_audience: This document is intended for developers and maintainers of the ArchSpine mirror system who need to customize or understand the code extraction rules. It provides the formal specification for mapping source code constructs to mirror entities.
key_takeaways: The document defines a pattern language for code extraction
It covers imports, exports (functions, structs, traits, impls, variables), and usages (calls, paths)
The patterns rely on tree-sitter node types and query syntax
It serves as a schema that drives the ArchSpine's ability to create a mirrored representation of code.
